Title: Carbon supply and water status regulate fatty acid and triacylglycerol biosynthesis at transcriptional level in the olive mesocarp

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the final acylation of diacylglycerol using acyl-CoA to produce triacylglycerol.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed/fruit storage oil; contributes to olive oil yield and quality
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in olive mesocarp at 20 WAF; increased under water stress in control fruit; reduced under girdled/defoliated treatment in water-stressed fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mesocarp at active oil accumulation stage (20 WAF); transcript upregulated by water stress in control fruit mesocarp
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key terminal TAG-synthesis gene supported; not explicitly established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: altered carbon supply (darkened fruit; girdled and defoliated shoot); well-watered versus water-stressed conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR transcript profiling under altered carbon-source and irrigation treatments; mesocarp oil content measurement; correlation analysis between expression and oil content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with promoting TAG biosynthesis and oil accumulation; transcript level increases under water stress and decreases when carbon supply is strongly limited
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeDGAT1-1 was implicated in TAG biosynthesis in olive mesocarp. 2. Under water stress, its transcript level increased in control fruit mesocarp relative to well-watered conditions. 3. Under carbon-limiting girdled/defoliated conditions in water-stressed fruit, its expression decreased together with reduced oil content.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression of OeDGAT1-1 responded mainly under water-stressed conditions; contribution under well-watered altered-carbon treatments was less evident than for OeDGAT2 and OePDAT1-2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate marker/gene for improving olive mesocarp oil accumulation and stability under water deficit; useful in marker-assisted selection and irrigation-responsive breeding

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes acyl-CoA-dependent acylation of diacylglycerol to form triacylglycerol.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: storage oil contributing to fruit oil yield
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: detected in mesocarp at 20 WAF; significantly increased under water stress in control and girdled/defoliated treatments
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mesocarp, especially responsive to water stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under irrigation and carbon-source treatments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: transcriptionally upregulated by water stress, suggesting a role in maintaining TAG synthesis under drought-related conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeDGAT1-2 expression was significantly increased by water stress in control fruit mesocarp. 2. Its transcript level also increased under water stress in girdled/defoliated fruit. 3. The paper emphasizes stress responsiveness rather than direct correlation as a principal TAG determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results and Conclusions identify it as transcriptionally upregulated by water stress, although it was not among the principal genes highlighted for well-watered TAG biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential marker for drought-responsive oil biosynthesis capacity in olive

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the final acyl-CoA-dependent acylation of diacylglycerol to yield triacylglycerol.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: storage oil; determinant of olive oil accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly responsive in mesocarp at 20 WAF; decreased under girdled/defoliated treatment in well-watered fruit and under both darkened and girdled/defoliated treatments in water-stressed fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mesocarp during active oil accumulation stage
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as major terminal determinant under well-watered conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR transcript profiling; mesocarp oil quantification; treatment-dependent expression–phenotype correlation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strong positive association with TAG biosynthesis and oil accumulation; reduced expression tracks reduced mesocarp oil under carbon limitation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeDGAT2 was one of the principal genes responsible for TAG biosynthesis in olive mesocarp. 2. Its transcript level decreased in girdled/defoliated fruit under well-watered conditions, paralleling reduced oil content. 3. Under water stress, expression also decreased in darkened and girdled/defoliated fruit, supporting a central role in carbon-supply-dependent oil accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights OeDGAT2 as having a major role, together with OePDAT1-2, under well-watered conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value target for breeding or engineering higher mesocarp oil accumulation, especially under favorable irrigation conditions

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from phospholipid, typically phosphatidylcholine, to diacylglycerol to produce triacylglycerol.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: detected in mesocarp at 20 WAF; strongly decreased in darkened and girdled/defoliated fruit under water stress; strongly increased by water stress in control fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mesocarp, responsive to water stress and carbon limitation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key terminal TAG-synthesis contributor; not established as sole bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR transcript profiling; oil content analysis; expression–phenotype correspondence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports TAG synthesis and oil accumulation, especially under stressed carbon/water regimes; upregulated by water stress in control fruit
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OePDAT1-1 was implicated in TAG biosynthesis in olive mesocarp. 2. Its expression decreased strongly in darkened and girdled/defoliated fruit under water stress, paralleling reduced oil content. 3. It was strongly upregulated by water stress in control fruit mesocarp.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports cooperation between DGAT and PDAT routes to guarantee TAG synthesis under altered carbon supply and water stress.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for maintaining oil accumulation under drought or altered carbon availability

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl moiety from phosphatidylcholine to diacylglycerol to form triacylglycerol.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: inferred from Results/Discussion as reduced in girdled/defoliated fruit under well-watered conditions and in darkened fruit under water stress, correlating with reduced oil content
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as major terminal determinant under well-watered conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR transcript profiling referenced in Results/Discussion; oil content correlation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: principal positive contributor to TAG biosynthesis and oil accumulation, especially under well-watered conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OePDAT1-2 was identified as one of the principal genes responsible for TAG biosynthesis in olive mesocarp. 2. Its reduced transcript level paralleled lower oil content under carbon-limiting treatments. 3. The paper indicates a major role for OePDAT1-2 under well-watered conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although Figure 5 description emphasizes significant changes mainly for PDAT1-1, the Discussion and Conclusions explicitly implicate PDAT1-2 as a principal TAG-biosynthesis gene with major role in well-watered conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for improving oil yield under non-stress conditions and for marker-assisted selection

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: microsomal oleic acid to linoleic acid desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: oleic acid moiety in membrane glycerolipids/phosphatidylcholine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a second double bond into oleate esterified in membrane lipids to generate linoleate.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor and contributes directly to target unsaturated fatty acid pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: contributes to olive oil quality and provides precursors for stress-related oxylipins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: increased in girdled/defoliated fruit mesocarp under well-watered conditions; also increased in darkened fruit under water stress relative to well-watered
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mesocarp at 20 WAF, responsive to altered carbon supply
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key determinant of unsaturated fatty acid composition in this study context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: altered carbon supply; water stress comparison
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; fatty acid composition profiling; expression–composition correlation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes increased polyunsaturated fatty acid proportion and reduced oleic acid under altered carbon supply
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeFAD2-1 expression increased in girdled/defoliated fruit under well-watered conditions. 2. This increase correlated with reduced oleic acid and increased linoleic/linolenic acids in mesocarp. 3. It was identified as one of the desaturase genes responsible for altered fatty acid composition under modified carbon supply.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: may reduce oleic acid proportion while increasing polyunsaturated fatty acids, affecting oil stability
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not detected as water-stress-upregulated in control fruit; role is mainly linked to altered carbon supply response.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: target for tuning olive oil unsaturation profile, balancing nutritional quality versus oxidative stability

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: microsomal oleic acid to linoleic acid desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: oleic acid moiety in membrane lipids/phosphatidylcholine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates oleate to linoleate in ER membrane lipids.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly contributes to target unsaturated fatty acid pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: oil quality determinant; precursor supply for stress signaling lipids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: increased in darkened and especially girdled/defoliated fruit under well-watered conditions; increased in girdled/defoliated fruit under water stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mesocarp at 20 WAF under altered carbon supply
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key determinant of linoleic acid composition in mesocarp
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: altered carbon supply; irrigation regime comparison
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes accumulation of polyunsaturated fatty acids and reduction of oleic acid under altered carbon supply
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeFAD2-2 transcript abundance rose under modified carbon supply, especially in girdled/defoliated fruit. 2. Increased expression correlated with higher linoleic/linolenic acids and lower oleic acid. 3. It was identified as a main desaturase gene responsive to altered carbon availability in mesocarp.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: elevated polyunsaturation may lower oxidative stability of olive oil
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Prior literature cited it as a major determinant of mesocarp linoleic acid; this study reinforces its responsiveness to carbon source changes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important target for tailoring fatty acid composition and oil quality in breeding

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: microsomal oleic acid to linoleic acid desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: oleic acid moiety in membrane lipids/phosphatidylcholine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates oleate to linoleate in microsomal membranes.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: contributes to target unsaturated fatty acid pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: oil quality and stress-response lipid precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in control fruit mesocarp, transcript level increased strongly under water stress relative to well-watered; reduced in darkened and girdled/defoliated fruit under water stress compared with control
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; highlighted as water-supply-regulated FAD2 member
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: transcriptionally upregulated by water stress, suggesting participation in water-status-dependent regulation of fatty acid desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeFAD2-5 expression increased strongly in control fruit mesocarp under water stress. 2. The paper suggests OeFAD2-5 is the olive FAD2 gene regulated by water supply. 3. Its expression decreased in darkened and girdled/defoliated fruit under water stress relative to control.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: possible increase in polyunsaturation may influence oil stability
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included primarily as a water-stress-responsive desaturase rather than as the main carbon-supply-responsive determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate marker for irrigation-responsive oil quality traits

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastidial linoleic acid to linolenic acid desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: linoleic acid moiety in plastid membrane lipids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a third double bond into linoleate in plastid lipids to generate linolenate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: contributes to olive oil composition and supplies precursors for jasmonate/oxylipin stress signaling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: significantly increased in girdled/defoliated fruit mesocarp under water stress relative to well-watered conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mesocarp at 20 WAF; responsive to altered carbon supply under water stress
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key determinant of linolenic acid changes in this study context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with increased linolenic acid proportion in response to altered carbon supply, especially under water stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeFAD7-1 expression increased in girdled/defoliated fruit under water stress. 2. Its elevated transcript level paralleled increased linolenic acid content. 3. The gene was identified as a main contributor to altered unsaturated fatty acid composition under modified carbon supply.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: higher linolenic acid may modify flavor and oxidative properties of oil
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No significant differences among carbon-source treatments within a single irrigation regime were detected overall, but the WW-versus-WS comparison in girdled fruit revealed significant induction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for adjusting linolenic acid and stress-related lipid traits in olive oil breeding
